Sitani Daley Gins.<|sq|>hracbih em D Resbozione9 I, when I announced the plan of business this morning, my plan of business is to consider the report of the working group, that's what I told you. After consideration of the report of the working group, I wore to the drafting group. I gave Brazil the opportunity to speak because of the extraordinary circumstances yesterday. Which I think we are not addressed. They have expressed their concerns of a sentimental apology. SG has clarified and I saw one also clarified on that aspect. And I turned Brazil for clarifying our, he says, our negotiations outside is not, he's not talking about discussions within the building, but I'm sure those that have been around animal for a long time, usually, there's this corridor diplomat, say people talk to one another. As he talked to friends, they said, okay, I want to go talk to a captain, a lot of kind of things. They know we want to know what's happening out there. When they say come turn into the hall. So with that said, what I'm going to do now is conclude the working group report. That is going to the actual items. When we feel the actual items, we'll come to the drafting group report and then we would consider the motion by Brazil. That's what we're going to do. So with that, I'd like to not turn the committee's attention back to the working group report and enjoy our attention to action, request the half of the committee. The committee is invited to approve the report of the group in general. Any particular one approved a draft work plan to prepare for the entry into the rules of the animal that's your framework. Ask the paragraphs full to 16 and annex one of the report. Subject two, abdication of the revised, mapple, annex six, eight, 25. May and wife. May and wife can ever buy the police drop their cards because I think it's an acceptable it is about this report. I would like to clarify the truth. Drop your cards. Let's consider our action items and general will come back to this issue. Your, when you can, you'll take the floor. Thank you. The flags are remaining. I assume it is on the action item requested. All right. So, action item one, where we're supposed to approve. I see further Arabia. Thank you very much, Chair. So we, we thank you for managing this difficult session. We're really happy to see that you're so healthy and standing here among us. It's the last day, so we think you'll make it through in the end. Hopefully so do we. But if I understand your intervention, sorry, your summation correctly, we are to consider these action items and then then move on to the drafting group. And then you said to consider the motion by Brazil. All right. Which is, if I understood correctly, as per the rules calling for a vote, on the matters of the explicit and tacit acceptance procedure and a vote on the adoption. Well, I would also like to propose a motion which sequentially would need to, as a necessary condition, come before the motion of Brazil. And this motion isn't support of the intervention of Singapore. We would like to give this house the opportunity to choose unity or choose division. So we would actually call for a roll call, vote, a motion. Do you accept to postpone the consideration of the adoption of the revised Marple I-6 until decided otherwise by the committee? Yes or no? This would necessarily need to come before the motion of Brazil sequentially. The option to choose unity or to choose division and allow us to be very clear in this moment. The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia chooses unity, but we are prepared and unshaken in the case that we want to go through division. We are prepared. This is what we will say. But we want to choose unity for the sake of the multilateral process, for the sake of the future of the IMO, for the sake of how we take work forward in this organization across issues, to avoid setting the dangerous precedent of division. We have made this clear since the first intervention we have made in this room, that we do not want to see a divided house. We want a united house. So we choose unity. We invite other members in this room to choose unity with us. And we move forward together as one group. Thank you very much. Thank you very much. Thank you very much. Thank you very much. Thank you very much. Thank you very much. Okay. Based on the motion back by Saudi Arabia, I'm going directly to a room that I want of the rooms of the city of the organization. There are one two areas. I mentioned to speak on a motion following with InRudh, there are one one above, Shabby Granta, only to the proposal. Saudi Arabia in the streets. And in addition to one speaker in favor of and two against the motion, after which a Shabby put in middle of the room to the food. So that's the room. So based on this, can everybody drop their flag? Please drop your flags because we need to get. And in addition to Saudi Arabia and motion, what we need is one speaker out in favor of and two against the motion before we put it to a vote. So. Okay. So this. All right. So I see. Take the photo for you. Okay. You know, you're going to take the photo from. Dema, can you request the floor? Yes. So. Dema, please. Thank you. Yes. We feel like we need to come in here with a point of. We need to come in here with a point of order as. Things are happening in a very strange way right now. As we consider the Brazil intervention that was a motion for a vote call for a vote, which we would assume would require the same procedure as has just been pointed to. So in the rules of procedure, so one country for two against this has happened already. I don't know why this wasn't reacted on. Now we have a motion from Saudi Arabia on a postponement and. Then it is considered. Then that is considered as a motion. We need to clear the speaking list that is not happening. That is why we call this point of order. Thank you, Denmark. And there are many please. Thank you. Thank you. Thank you. —We have already reached the end of the day. —Our 9-gaybli fleet, Director of Liga, Engelstinnar, first of all, explain the rules to us. —Okay. —All right, please. —Thank you, Mr. Chair. Good morning. This thing which delegates we are right now in Rule 31. There was a proposal to have a vote, which was then followed by the proposal to defer the debate, to adjourn the debate. 30-30, 1-31, 1-31, 1-31, 1-31, 1-31, 1-31, 1-4. As the chair explained, there should be one speaker in favor and two against the motion. After which, it should be put immediately to the vote. Thank you. Thank you. So, with that, we will not continue with the motion, but Saudi, I hope that clarifies. So, I need cards for one for and one for two against two. United, please. I thank the Secretary for the explanation and pointing to rule 31, specifically 31.3 and 31.4, which is what we were looking at. But the question is, what is the motion we are, we should be voting on. As I understood, the agenda and the debate that we've been having this week and as the chair outlined this morning, the order of discussion was to be reported, the working group, reported the drafting group, and then proceed to adoption. And that's what we've all expected, that's standard for the meetings. We understand that there are proposals on the floor to sort of jump straight to votes on delay or votes on adoption. But it seems the part of the agenda that we are in is the discussion of the working group report. So, the correct motion would be to adjourn 31.3, to adjourn the debate on the question under discussion, the question under discussion is the working group report. And so, if there is to be a motion now to close that, that seems the upper down motion and item for speakers to address, not the question of delay or adoption. Thank you. And the point that we are making here is that the membership clearly, we began this with appeal to transparency. The membership, there are 17 cards up. The membership clearly wants to speak on the issue of what happened last night and the discussion that occurred outside of the room. And the Singaporean proposal is clearly an expression of the will of the membership to at least speak on that. That's democratic, that's transparent. The United States, if we are going by the rule of procedure, the specificities that we should immediately address, the motion that has been taken, has been done by Saudi Arabia, has been done. We have to address that first. Now, if the decision is not to postpone, then of course we continue with taking the drafting group report and then both the drafting group and the working group report. Because this matter that has to be dealt with immediately, the aspect of the rule of procedure, so we now have to take it off the way before we come back to consideration of the action items on the report of the working group and drafting group. So that's all we are going to do now. So I'm not here in U.S. Yes. What on? Sorry. So that would be under point one. Where is the rule about immediate attention taken preceding? So I move that you make a decision first on the first proposal. Thank you. I will rewrite.
